html:
Victory for Technology is a small, newly visible Taiwan functional-film coater, not the etched-foil AI capacitor story in the prior draft. The official product mix is still overwhelmingly optical protection film: 2024 revenue was TWD 581 million, with TWD 575 million, or 99%, from optical protection film and only 1% from “other.” Its own site lists PI/CPI film, PET/PE process films, diffuser film, aluminum-based foil, etched cathode foil and forming foil, but the disclosed economics are not yet a foil business. The real long thesis is narrower and more fragile, but still interesting: a customer-qualified process-film supplier for high-end tablets, foldables, AR/VR/MR, AI hardware manufacturing and potentially semiconductor DAF materials that is trying to move from a sub-TWD 1 billion niche supplier into a higher-value electronic materials platform.

The operating improvement is real. Revenue went from TWD 343 million in 2023 to TWD 581 million in 2024 and TWD 706 million in 2025. EPS moved from TWD 0.29 to TWD 3.62 to TWD 5.90. Gross margin expanded to 24.2% in 2025 and operating margin to roughly 11.2%, with net income of TWD 70.7 million. That is a genuine inflection, not an accounting mirage. The 2024 shareholder letter also says high-polymer materials were successfully introduced into AI hardware manufacturing process applications and entered mass production, while the company’s forward plan names foldable electronics, AR/VR/MR, AI and semiconductor material applications.

The variant perception is that Victory is not a generic display-material recycler from the old touch-panel cycle. Its better products are customized process materials that sit inside customer manufacturing workflows. MoneyDJ’s company profile, citing public company materials, says the company’s three-layer composite film was used in Apple iPad projects and that Victory had an estimated 44% share of optical films used in tablet production processes in 2023. That kind of position is not easily visible in a Bloomberg-style product label, but it matters: qualification, cleanliness, adhesive formulation, release behavior, heat resistance and customer co-development can create stickiness even if the headline product sounds like “film.”

The problem is valuation. At about TWD 240 per share, with 12 million shares outstanding, the market cap is around TWD 2.9 billion, or roughly USD 91 million. That is about 41x 2025 EPS of TWD 5.90 and more than 12x book value using recent BPS around TWD 18.5. This is no longer an ignored cheap micro-cap. It was discovered hard: the stock has traded as high as TWD 296 versus a 52-week low near TWD 72, and it was flagged as an attention stock after an extreme five-day move. The share register is also very tight, with directors near 50%, one large holder around 40%, foreign ownership near 1%, and only about 500 shareholders recently shown. That supports upside torque, but it also means price can overshoot fundamentals in both directions.

The latest revenue data forces discipline. January 2026 revenue was up 11.6% year over year, March was up 15.5%, but February fell 44.6% and April fell 30.6%. Through April, 2026 revenue was TWD 193.6 million, down 14.0% year over year. If the company is truly entering a new AI/semi/foldable materials cycle, that weakness needs to reverse by midyear. If it does not, the market is capitalizing 2025 peak earnings rather than forward inflection.

The expansion plan is the key tell. Victory approved a Tainan Qigu plant project with an estimated budget around TWD 585 million, a huge amount versus 2025 revenue and book equity. It is also issuing 2 million new shares, about 16.7% of the current share count, and paying a TWD 5.6 cash dividend from 2025 earnings. This is not a net-cash downside-protection story. It is a capital allocation bet: management is paying shareholders, raising equity, and building capacity for new materials before the revenue ramp is fully proven. If the new plant is filled with AI hardware process films, flexible/foldable materials and semiconductor DAF materials, the current valuation can still work. If it is just more optical protection film capacity, the stock is too expensive.

The best bull case is that 2026 is a noisy transition year before a 2027 step-up. Under that version, the 2024-2025 earnings lift was only the first proof point, AR film and high-polymer AI process materials broaden customer wallet share, and the new factory gives Victory capacity for higher-spec materials rather than commodity film. A business doing TWD 1.2 billion to TWD 1.4 billion of revenue at mid-to-high 20s gross margin and 15% to 18% net margin could earn TWD 14 to TWD 18 per share even after dilution. For a tiny, high-ROE, customer-qualified AI/foldable/semi materials supplier, a 24x to 28x multiple would not be absurd.

The base case is more restrained. Revenue recovers in H2 but 2026 remains roughly flat to modestly up versus 2025, with EPS around TWD 5.5 to TWD 6.5 before full dilution pressure. The market continues to give Victory a premium because the option value is real, but not enough to justify a large move until monthly revenue confirms a new leg. That leaves the stock fairly valued to slightly undervalued only if investors are willing to look into 2027.

The bear case is straightforward: the 2025 earnings surge was a customer/product cycle, not a structural reset. Monthly revenue weakness continues, the Tainan plant absorbs cash before it contributes, the rights issue dilutes holders, the withdrawn OTC listing application delays institutional sponsorship, and the stock de-rates from “AI materials option” back to “small optical film coater.” The profitable core and customer qualifications prevent a zero, but they do not protect a 40x earnings multiple.

Bear target: TWD 110, about 54% downside from TWD 240. This assumes EPS falls toward TWD 4.5 to TWD 5.0 and the market applies a low-20s multiple to a small cyclical optical-materials company.

Base target: TWD 260, about 8% upside. This assumes 2026 revenue stabilizes, EPS lands around TWD 6, and the market keeps paying a premium for the 2027 new-materials option.

Bull target: TWD 420, about 75% upside. This requires visible AI/foldable/semi material revenue, post-dilution EPS power of roughly TWD 16, and a 26x multiple for a newly proven specialty electronic-materials growth story.
